items.title: Optimize Builder Scheduling with Anti-Affinity in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the CPU request for the builder pod to a more manageable level, addressing the issue of the pod being stuck in a Pending state. Additionally, a soft pod anti-affinity rule has been implemented to prevent disk I/O contention by avoiding nodes already running tenant QEMU instances. These changes enhance the overall scheduling efficiency and resource utilization in the cluster.

items.title: Optimize builder node allocation for smoother installs in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update dedicates a node to the builder to prevent KVM starvation during fresh installs. By increasing the builder's CPU and memory requests, the installation process now completes efficiently before the OOBE finalizes, ensuring a successful setup. The changes were validated end-to-end, improving overall performance.

items.title: Fix fresh golden builds and enhance diagnostics in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where fresh golden builds failed to start due to a command parse crash in the install script. Key changes include direct launching of startup scripts and the addition of a log-forwarding script for better diagnostics. The health-gate mechanism has also been improved to ensure reliable monitoring of the builder's status.

items.title: Streamline Auth Iframe Login Logic in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the authentication process by removing the reliance on the feature flag for the embedded auth-iframe login. Instead, the login method is now determined by the `LOGIN_METHOD` cookie, defaulting to a full-page redirect unless explicitly set to use the iframe. Key changes include the introduction of new utility functions for managing login preferences and updates to the router logic for handling login routes.

items.title: Revamp User Dropdown for Consistency with Nexior in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: The top-right user dropdown has been redesigned to align visually with the Nexior menu. Key updates include the addition of an account header with an avatar and email, a divider for better separation, and FontAwesome icons for each action. The dropdown's behavior remains unchanged, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: Empower Site Admins with Per-Service Pricing Controls in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Services' settings tab for site admins, allowing them to configure per-service pricing and display options directly within the app. Key features include editable markup previews, service visibility toggles, and enhanced internationalization support across 18 locales. The changes streamline the management of service settings, making it easier for admins to customize their offerings.

items.title: Align per-service markup cap to site-wide limit in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update lowers the per-service markup ceiling from 100 to 5, aligning it with the site-wide cap. This change simplifies the pricing structure and eliminates confusion by ensuring all components of the stack adhere to the same markup limit. The implementation has been verified against production data, confirming no existing entries exceed the new limit.

items.title: Revamped Pricing Tab and Money Preview for Enhanced UX in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update restores the site-wide Pricing tab, allowing affiliate admins to configure markup with live previews. It also unifies the markup cap across site-wide and per-service settings, ensuring consistency. Inline validation and internationalization improvements enhance usability across multiple languages.

items.title: New Bank Transfer Payment Option Added in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced a new payment method for bank transfers on the console order details page, which is gated by the `enableBankTransfer` feature flag and is hidden by default. This option is available for orders of $10,000 or more, providing users with essential account information and guidelines for successful transactions. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for all 12 languages.

items.title: Clean Up Service Tier in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `service_tier` parameter from the seedance API as per the latest API spec changes. Key modifications include the deletion of the `_SERVICE_TIERS` constant and the removal of related options in the video command. Additionally, tests have been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring that `service_tier` is no longer part of the payload.

items.title: New USD Bank Transfer Payment Method for Large Orders in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new enterprise USD bank-transfer payment method for orders exceeding $10,000. The payment process requires buyers to wire funds to our Mercury account, generating a unique reference code for tracking. The order remains in a PENDING state until fulfilled by a superuser, ensuring secure handling of large transactions.

items.title: Retire service_tier parameter from API spec in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `service_tier` parameter from the API specification and documentation, as it was not effectively integrated into the cost rules and caused confusion for users. Key changes include the deletion of related properties in the OpenAPI JSON and documentation files. This cleanup enhances clarity and prevents potential customer-facing issues.

items.title: Eliminate Flex Tier to Boost Seedance Performance in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the 'flex' service tier from the Seedance configuration, which was causing timeout issues during video generation. By defaulting to the 'realtime' tier, users will experience faster processing times and improved reliability. Key changes include the removal of the ServiceTier selector and adjustments to the API request handling to ensure the 'flex' tier is not used.

items.title: Retire service_tier parameter for smoother video generation in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update removes the `service_tier` parameter from the Volcengine seedance worker, addressing customer-reported timeouts during video generation. All requests will now default to the realtime tier, ensuring faster processing times. Key changes include modifications to the request handling and core logic to eliminate the parameter entirely.

items.title: Enhance Login Experience with Configurable Modes in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a new feature allowing users to choose their preferred login method in the settings. Users can select between an embedded iframe popup or a full-page redirect, with their choice saved in a persistent cookie. This change improves user experience by making the login method more accessible and customizable.

items.title: Strengthening Logo Integrity in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a critical fix to prevent the use of fabricated brand logos in Maestro videos. A new rule mandates sourcing official logos instead of hand-drawing or generating them, addressing a previously overlooked fabrication vector. The changes enhance fidelity checks and ensure that only verified brand marks are used, significantly improving the quality and authenticity of video content.

items.title: Enhance Mercury USD Channel with New Features in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Mercury USD international wire transfer channel, implementing a $10,000 minimum threshold, mandatory reference codes, and customer support fallback strategies. Key documents have been added to outline the integration and eligibility gating processes, ensuring a robust framework for handling transactions. No production code changes were made, focusing solely on documentation enhancements.

items.title: Enhance server-side auth-iframe support in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the issue where the server-side `auth-iframe` feature flag was ignored, ensuring that in-app iframe login works as intended. Key changes include the addition of a fallback mechanism to read server flags and the preservation of client-side opt-outs. The validation process confirmed that the new logic functions correctly without introducing regressions.

items.title: Fix iOS App Store Submission Issues in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where the iOS App Store auto-submit was failing due to missing required release notes. Key changes include setting the 'whatsNew' notes for submissions, cleaning up stale drafts, and ensuring that submission failures are now visibly flagged. These improvements will enhance the reliability of the submission process and prevent future freezes in the App Store version.

items.title: Revamp WeChat Login UI for a Sleeker Look in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances the WeChat login interface by transforming the previously stark white iframe into a designed scan card with rounded corners and a subtle shadow. The iframe's width has been adjusted to prevent overflow while maintaining a clean aesthetic. These changes improve the visual experience without altering the underlying login logic.

items.title: Enhance Narration Budgeting for Maestro in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update introduces a deterministic narration budget for the Maestro video rendering process. Key changes include enforcing a script length that matches the requested duration, preventing issues with overlong narrations that previously caused render timeouts. Additionally, the delivery and resume prompts have been updated to ensure proper validation of completed renders, enhancing overall reliability.

items.title: Enhance Maestro with Trace ID and Canonical Task IDs in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces canonical UUID strings for Maestro task IDs, improving clarity and consistency. Additionally, the top-level `trace_id` is now included in the responses of `POST /maestro/tasks`, allowing clients to better track gateway traces. Focused tests have been added to ensure proper functionality of these features.

items.title: Fix SPA Hang on Initial Navigation with Auth Iframe in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where the SPA would hang on a blank loading spinner for users with the auth-iframe feature enabled. The fix ensures that the initial navigation is no longer aborted, allowing the app to render properly. Additionally, a new mechanism has been implemented to handle the overlay display without race conditions, improving user experience during authentication.

items.title: Ensure Narration Voice is Hard-Pinned in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update fixes the issue where the requested narration voice was not being applied correctly in Maestro. By hard-pinning the voice in the environment, the system now guarantees that the specified voice takes precedence over any agent suggestions. This change enhances the reliability of audio outputs for narration tasks without affecting multi-character voice settings.

items.title: Update Maestro Bot Logo in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update changes the Maestro bot logo to the new Holo Window design by modifying the `MAESTRO_LOGO` constant. The new logo URL is now set to `https://cdn.acedata.cloud/df0e7b7eea.svg`, ensuring consistency across the platform. Additionally, the update includes validation checks to confirm the logo's functionality.

items.title: Update Holo logo assets for Maestro in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances Maestro's public display by implementing the new Holo Window redesign. Key changes include updated URLs for the service and document icons, ensuring distinct assets for both the small icon and wider thumbnail. The migration has been validated for consistency and functionality.

items.title: Enhance Suno Voice Clone Audio Guidelines in Backend 🎤
items.summary: This update clarifies the audio requirements for the Suno voice clone feature by consolidating key information into a single blockquote. It details accepted audio formats, minimum duration, and specific voice characteristics while addressing potential risks associated with noise and multi-speaker scenarios. This change improves the documentation's clarity and usability for developers.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Profile Retrieval After Login in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a background task to backfill the WeChat profile (nickname, wechat_id, avatar) after a post-startup login. Key changes include a new method to read profile data directly from the local WeChat database, eliminating the need for UI interaction. The implementation has been tested thoroughly, ensuring that profiles are populated promptly and accurately.

items.title: Align Builder and Preview Container Environment in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a boot-loop issue during fresh installations by aligning the builder and preview container environment variables with the standardized wechat-bot Service values. Key changes include updating the VERSION to '10' and setting the LANGUAGE, REGION, and KEYBOARD to 'Chinese' and 'zh-CN'. These adjustments ensure that fresh golden builds complete successfully and the API becomes healthy.

items.title: Streamline gpt-image-2 Reverse Pool for Better Performance in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the `gpt-image-2` reverse pool by limiting it to only `br` and `br-fallback`, eliminating the slow `ttapi` and `ephone` channels. Analysis showed that the removed channels rarely contributed to successful requests, ensuring that high-resolution requests are prioritized. The change enhances overall efficiency and aligns with customer expectations for true 4K quality.

items.title: Revamped WeChat Desktop Experience in Wisdom 🎉
items.summary: This update significantly enhances the web interface to mirror the WeChat desktop experience, addressing six user requests including UI and API optimizations. Key changes include a redesigned layout, improved API integrations, and the introduction of new features like Moments and group management. The overall user experience is now more intuitive and responsive, ensuring seamless interactions.

items.title: Enhance WeChat API Test Endpoints in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces four new read-only GET endpoints for the WeChat appliance API test dropdown on the deployment management page. Users can now access endpoints for contacts, conversations, messages, and moments, enhancing the testing capabilities. Additionally, the dropdown allows for custom path entry, improving user flexibility in API testing.

items.title: Revamp CLI to Align with Updated API in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the `veo` CLI package with the latest API documentation by removing outdated commands and simplifying the `upscale` functionality to support only 1080p. Key changes include the removal of legacy commands and models, as well as updates to tests and documentation to reflect the current API contract. The command guidance has been clarified to enhance user experience.

items.title: Enhance gpt-image-2 routing to ensure high-res output in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the issue of low-resolution outputs from the gpt-image-2 model by removing it from the cortex channel and introducing a new fallback channel. The routing now prioritizes high-resolution images by utilizing a dual bananarouter setup, ensuring that requests retry on a second channel instead of degrading to a lower quality. The official relay remains unchanged, maintaining the integrity of other models served by cortex.

items.title: Revamped Video Type Thumbnails for a Cohesive Look in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update regenerates the Video Type thumbnails into a cohesive set of 3:4 portrait images, ensuring each type is easily recognizable at a glance. The new thumbnails eliminate cropping issues, featuring subjects in the upper frame for a polished appearance. Only the thumbnail URLs in the constants file were modified, with no impact on logic or layout.

items.title: Retire Veo Ops Actions for a Cleaner UI in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the Veo post-processing actions from the Nexior UI, streamlining the user experience as Veo transitions to a generation-only model. Key components related to upsample, extend, reshoot, and object actions have been deleted, resulting in a total of 1597 deletions while maintaining the generation flow intact. All relevant internationalization keys have also been removed symmetrically across all locales.

items.title: Boosted Pricing and API Retirement for Veo in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update raises the pricing for Veo's quality and fast tiers by approximately 1.5x, aligning with the upstream TTAPI changes. Additionally, four Veo operations APIs have been deprecated and soft-hidden, ensuring existing integrations remain functional while removing them from public documentation. The migration is reversible, and all related tests have passed successfully.

items.title: Exciting New Veo Video Generation Feature in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces Veo video generation to the ttapi upstream worker, allowing the `/veo/videos` endpoint to serve responses identical to the existing Veo provider. Key additions include a new handler for video requests and modifications to core functionalities to facilitate video generation and fetching. The rollout is additive and safe, ensuring no immediate traffic changes until further routing adjustments are made.

items.title: Enhance Translation CronJob Resilience in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update strengthens the `platform-backend-translation` CronJob by increasing memory limits and adjusting job history retention settings. The memory limit is raised to 768Mi to prevent OOM crashes, while the failed jobs history limit is increased to 3 to aid in debugging. Additionally, finished jobs will now auto-delete after one day to avoid stale entries, enhancing overall system reliability.

items.title: Clickable Image Thumbnails for Full-Screen Preview in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: We've enhanced the user experience by making all image thumbnails in task history and result cards clickable, allowing users to open a full-screen preview lightbox. This update applies across various image and video services, ensuring a consistent and intuitive interaction. Key components have been modified to support this feature while preserving existing functionalities like upload progress and error handling.

items.title: Extended health-gate timeout for smoother installs in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: The health-gate timeout for the snapshot job has been increased from 90 minutes to 180 minutes to accommodate fresh Windows installs on contended KVM nodes. This change ensures that golden-build runs can complete end-to-end without manual intervention, improving overall reliability during the process. The adjustments were made following observations during a live end-to-end run.

items.title: Enhance Video Type Cards for Better Visibility in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the display of Video Type cards in the Maestro config panel by changing the thumbnail aspect ratio from 16:9 to 3:4. The new compact vertical design ensures that subjects' heads are fully visible, enhancing clarity and aesthetics. No image regeneration is necessary as the original photos contain the full subjects.

items.title: Revamped Maestro API Integration Guides in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The integration guides for the Maestro API have been completely rewritten to align with the house style of other API documentation. Key updates include detailed sections on the application process, usage examples in CURL and Python, and accurate billing information. These changes enhance clarity and usability for developers integrating with the Maestro service.

items.title: Restore cinematic service thumbnail in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update restores the cinematic service thumbnail for the Maestro service, which was previously replaced by a flat SVG logo due to a migration error. A new migration has been implemented to revert the thumbnail back to its original artwork while retaining the SVG logo for the icon. This ensures that the service's visual representation is accurate and appealing.

items.title: Standardize Maestro Task-Card Row Spacing in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update aligns the Maestro task-card metadata rows to match the established spacing conventions used in the nanobanana and kling previews. The changes include normalizing vertical gaps to `mb-2` for most rows and ensuring the last row has `mb-0`. This CSS-only adjustment enhances visual consistency without impacting functionality or data.

items.title: Improve Error Handling for Kling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances error handling in the Kling service by classifying certain input-fetch failures as 400 errors instead of 500, reducing unnecessary 5xx alerts. Additionally, it fixes a bug related to poll timeouts, ensuring that tasks that do not succeed will now correctly trigger a 504 TimeoutError. These changes improve the reliability and clarity of error reporting in the service.

items.title: Automate Golden-Image CI Workflows in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update implements the CI portion of the golden-image plan, featuring two new workflows: one for building and snapshotting the golden image, and another for promoting it. Key changes include the addition of YAML templates for deployment and validation steps, ensuring robust health checks and streamlined promotion processes. The enhancements will improve the efficiency and reliability of the golden-image deployment in the Wisdom environment.

items.title: Enhance golden snapshot resolution in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the golden snapshot resolution process by ensuring that the `golden-current` ConfigMap serves as the single source of truth for both image and snapshot. The changes streamline the deployment process, allowing for a more atomic and efficient promotion of golden images without the need for Service metadata edits. Additionally, tests have been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring robust functionality.

items.title: Update Maestro Document and Icon Alignment in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update keeps the `/documents/maestro` as an empty root document and aligns its icon with Nexior's logo. The changes also ensure that the migration graph is resolved by depending on the current `0204_*` migrations. A live hotfix has already been applied in production to reflect these updates.

items.title: Streamlined Integration Docs & Translated 4.8k Strings in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update shortens the 'Integration Docs' button to a more concise 'Docs' across multiple locales. Additionally, approximately 4,800 untranslated strings have been translated into 10 different languages, enhancing accessibility and user experience for a diverse audience.

items.title: Standardize Workload Provider to k8s_appliance in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a banner in the canonical plans to indicate the standardization of the `aks_appliance` workload provider to a vendor-neutral `k8s_appliance`. The change groups cluster-identifying fields under a `cluster` profile, clarifying that AKS is now treated as configuration rather than a provider. Historical references to `aks_appliance` are preserved for design record purposes.

items.title: Standardize AKS to K8s Appliance in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a key standardization in the Wisdom golden-snapshot design documentation, changing the `aks_appliance` provider to a vendor-neutral `k8s_appliance`. The modification includes a banner in the documentation that clarifies this shift, ensuring that AKS is now treated as a configuration rather than a provider. The historical decision regarding `aks_appliance` remains intact for reference.

items.title: Update Wisdom Docs for Provider Renaming in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the Wisdom deployment documentation with the recent standardization of the PlatformBackend provider, changing references from `aks_appliance` to `k8s_appliance`. Key modifications include updates to various documentation files and the deployment pipeline schema, ensuring clarity and consistency across the platform. No functional changes were made, as this is a documentation-only update.

items.title: Standardize appliance provider name to k8s_appliance in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the `aks_appliance` provider to `k8s_appliance` in the Manage.vue component, aligning with the PlatformBackend standardization. The `isApplianceWorkload` function now recognizes `k8s_appliance` as an appliance workload, ensuring that Stop/Start controls and WeChat QR/MCP channel cards are properly rendered for Kubernetes golden-snapshot tenants.

items.title: Standardize AKS to Vendor-Neutral K8s Provider in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update standardizes the deployment abstraction by renaming the `aks_appliance` to `k8s_appliance`, making it vendor-neutral. Key changes include restructuring cluster-identifying fields under a `cluster` profile block and updating various deployment scripts and tests accordingly. The migration ensures existing services are rewritten to the new format while maintaining a clean codebase with no references to the old provider.

items.title: Update Wisdom Appliances to QR-fix v5 in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update points the `wechat-bot` and `wisdom` AKS appliance services to the new QR-fix v5 image. The migration script has been added to ensure the correct image is used for new tenants, while existing services have been validated successfully. The deployment has been updated in AKS to reflect these changes.

items.title: Enhance WeChat 4 QR Navigation Support in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for WeChat 4's navigable login state for QR requests, improving user experience. Key changes include the addition of an owner-drawn 'Switch Account' click path and enhancements to the QR capture process using an OCR-aware service page detector. These modifications ensure consistent classification of WeChat 4 windows and prepare the system for future golden builds.

items.title: Enhance Maestro Document Relations in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an idempotent data migration to register and repair Maestro documents within the public development document tree. Key improvements include fixing document ranks, tags, and metadata, along with seeding translations for service labels. The migration has been rebased onto the latest main branch to ensure consistency and avoid conflicts.

items.title: Documented x402 Sync Result as No-Op in Clis 🚫
items.summary: This update confirms that the documentation-only changes for `x402` do not require any syncing actions within the Clis monorepo. No source files were modified, and it was verified that there is no corresponding package or entry for `x402`. The issue is now resolved as a no-op, ensuring clarity in the sync process.

items.title: Updated Company Address for Slack Marketplace Plan in Index 🚀
items.summary: The company address has been updated in the Slack marketplace plan from 201 North Broad St, Middletown, DE to 131 Continental Dr, Suite 305, Newark, DE 19713. This change ensures that users have the most accurate contact information. The update was made in the relevant documentation file.

items.title: Updated Company Address for All Locales in Docs 📍
items.summary: The company address for Germey Technology, LLC has been updated across 53 files in various locales, changing from 651 N Broad St, Suite 201, Middletown, Delaware to 131 Continental Dr, Suite 305, Newark, DE 19713. This update ensures that all documentation reflects the correct address for customer support and privacy information.

items.title: Optimize Application List Query for Performance in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a significant PostgreSQL CPU spike caused by inefficient querying in the Applications list endpoint. By restructuring the query to utilize indexed sub-queries instead of an OR-join, execution time has been reduced from 2539 ms to approximately 3 ms, greatly enhancing performance. Additionally, tests have been updated to reflect these changes and ensure continued reliability.

items.title: Retire Motion Graphics Workflow in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update fully retires the Motion Graphics workflow from the Maestro system, aligning backend routing with the UI. Key changes include the removal of the `motion` scenario from the flow registry and the deletion of the entire `skills/motion-graphics/` workflow. All related documentation has been scrubbed to reflect this change, ensuring a clean and consistent user experience.

items.title: Automate Wisdom Golden-Image CI Lifecycle in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive plan to automate the Wisdom golden-snapshot lifecycle through GitHub Actions, replacing manual processes with streamlined workflows. Key additions include new documentation for CI automation and a fix in the PlatformBackend provider to ensure proper image and snapshot handling. The implementation will follow in subsequent PRs, enhancing efficiency and reliability in deployment.

items.title: Retire Motion Graphics & Require Video for Captions in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update removes the Motion Graphics option from the Maestro video-type picker, as it was underperforming. Additionally, the Captions feature now requires an uploaded source video before generating captions, improving user experience by preventing confusion. Internationalization updates have been made to reflect these changes across all supported languages.

items.title: Enhance spacing for in-progress task cards in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the visual layout of the in-progress task card in Maestro by adjusting the row spacing for the request-params list and Task ID. The margin-bottom has been increased from 4px to 8px, aligning it with the success and failure card styles. This change is purely cosmetic, ensuring a more consistent and less cramped appearance across card states.

items.title: Enhance Seedance Model Mapping for Quality in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a refined mapping of the Maestro Seedance 2.0 r2v models to production quality tiers, ensuring that draft models are linked to mini, standard to fast, and premium to pro. The changes are applied to both short-drama and avatar workflows, incorporating retry and fallback budgets to prevent unintended upgrades. The validation process confirmed the consistency and alignment with Seedance 2.0 constraints.

items.title: Enhance Windows Viewer Access for AKS in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update exposes the dockur Windows viewer for AKS Wisdom and WeChat-bot appliances through a new ingress on port 8006, secured with Basic Auth. Key changes include the addition of migration 0201 to enable this feature on existing services and updates to deployment access URLs for application owners. The implementation has been validated with successful tests and thorough reviews.

items.title: Align WeChat Bot Dockur Environment in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a data migration to align the WeChat bot and Wisdom AKS appliance dockur environment with the verified Chinese Windows 10 golden snapshot. Key changes include setting the critical dockur base environment variables and updating the AKS appliance metadata schema to prevent stale values in future seed data. Validation confirms that the tenant successfully booted without issues, ensuring a smooth deployment.

items.title: Update macOS Desktop Icon for Better Visibility in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update regenerates the macOS desktop app icon assets to include an opaque white badge background, enhancing visibility. The Windows icon and shared assets have been synchronized with the new icon generator output. Additionally, desktop icon assets and generator scripts are now included in the workflow to ensure future changes are properly validated.

items.title: Updated Documentation to Reflect New Structure in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This PR synchronizes the documentation with the recent restructuring of the Wisdom project. Key updates include a rewritten README, updated architecture diagrams, and corrections across multiple documentation files to align with the new directory layout. These changes enhance clarity and ensure that users have accurate information about the project's structure and functionality.

items.title: Update qs dependency to enhance performance in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the qs library from version 6.15.1 to 6.15.3, introducing several fixes and performance improvements. Key changes include enhanced robustness in parsing and stringifying, as well as optimizations that make the utility functions more efficient. These updates will help ensure better handling of query strings in the application.
